item.title: Dual Tone Multi-Frequency Home Modulation System
item.description: Technology has advanced more and more over the years, and with this advancement, it has become an essential part of our lives. It has made our lives more comfortable and at the same time easier. We are able to access and do more work efficiently and much more easily without much human effort. Technology is substituting the manual work and everything we do in our day to day lives is becoming more automated.

item.title: Access Control using Radio Frequency Identification
item.description: In today's world we have a lot of products that go through a lot of distribution centers. We require something that can electronically manage all the products and store data, big data of the products that are going through these centers. Companies today want this data and it is very hard to manually keep track of every item. Auburn University's RFID Lab conducted research on the use of RFID technology for distribution centers like Walmart, etc.

item.title: About
item.description: Currently pursuing a Master of Science in Data Science for Public Policy student from Georgetown University's McCourt School of Public Policy, I conduct research in the area of security policy working as a Research Assistant for professors at Georgetown. Having completed a Bachelor of Engineering from the University of Mumbai majoring in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering I was also heavily inclined toward policy and hence actively participated in Model United Nations conferences during my undergraduate years.
